name: Khalaf ibn Muslim
title: Epic-Making Teenager
shortDescription: Muslim ibn Awsaja's teenage son who, after his father's martyrdom, went to the battlefield with his mother's encouragement and was bravely martyred.
fullStory:
Khalaf ibn Muslim ibn Awsaja was a brave and pious teenager in the Karbala caravan. His father, Muslim, was one of the greatest companions of the Prophet and Imam Ali (AS) who was martyred in the first hours of the Ashura battle.

After Muslim ibn Awsaja's martyrdom, Khalaf's mother (Umm Khalaf) told him: 'My son! Rise and hasten to help the Imam.' Imam Hussain (AS), who was compassionate towards the youth, said: 'If you are killed, your mother will be left alone.' But Umm Khalaf said: 'I myself commanded him to fight!'

With the Imam's permission, Khalaf entered the battlefield and, despite his young age, recited such an epic poem and wielded his sword with such skill that it amazed the enemies.

The Kufan soldiers, unable to withstand his sword, surrounded him. After killing several enemies, Khalaf was unjustly martyred. The enemies severed his head and threw it towards his mother, but his mother kissed his head and thanked God.
quoteArabic: أَنا خَلَفُ بْنُ مُسْلِمِ بْنِ عَوْسَجَةٍ...
quote: (He recited while fighting:) I am Khalaf ibn Muslim... a son from a lineage of brave men who strikes with his sword to defend the truth.
